factors.name: Dow Financials Leadership
factors.summary: GS +4.93%, JPM +3.64%, AXP +4.41% driving broad-based rally with 25 of 30 components higher. Dow up 930pts (1.84%) mid-session per InvestingLive, setting pace for record close.

factors.name: Sector Rotation Out of Semiconductors
factors.summary: Tech weakness (Broadcom disappointing, Micron under pressure) redirecting capital into cyclical financials and industrials—Dow's sweet spot. Real-economy rotation favors Dow's composition.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk Reduction
factors.summary: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire brokered by US, Putin signaling readiness for Ukraine deal, Trump reluctant to restart Iran war per WSJ. Oil down 3% on ceasefire hopes, reducing energy input cost inflation pressures.

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Tone Persists
factors.summary: Fed's Schmid (hawk) says inflation remains top risk after 5-year fight; Fed's Daly reiterates inflation priority despite policy being 'in a good place.' No dovish pivot signals yet.

factors.name: Treasury Market Stress Signals
factors.summary: BOE Bailey notes debt market leverage raises vulnerability questions; UK 10y rose 97bps Feb-May, US 10y up 76bps Mar-May. War-weary Treasury market facing fresh test with Friday jobs report—rate volatility risk.

factors.name: Labor Market Mixed Signals
factors.summary: Long-term unemployment surging per recent reports; Treasury Sec Bessent cryptic about job market ('wish it had come out today'). Friday's NFP will be critical inflection point—no clear directional bias yet.

factors.name: IPO/Capital Rotation Flows
factors.summary: Quantinuum +13%, Innio +15%, Liftoff +21%, Sunshine Silver +11% pop. Strong IPO demand suggests retail/institutional capital rotating into growth and speculative names, reducing equity market friction.

factors.name: Oil Price Stabilization
factors.summary: Oil snaps 3-day win streak on ceasefire, down ~3%; lower energy costs reduce input inflation for industrials and transportation (logistics), benefiting Dow cyclicals.

factors.name: Private Credit Redemption Risk
factors.summary: Blackstone capped $79B BCRED fund withdrawals after redemption requests surged from 7.9% to 10%. Signals liquidity stress in private markets—potential contagion risk if spreads widen.

factors.name: China/Global Growth Uncertainty
factors.summary: No fresh China PMI or growth data in this window. CAT, BA, DE exposure to China demand remains opaque; escalation in US-Iran tensions or trade policy could flip this negative.

screening.narrative: The Dow is in the midst of a broad-based rally (1.84% mid-session, 930pts) driven by rotation OUT of semiconductors and INTO financials and industrial cyclicals—precisely the Dow's DNA. Geopolitical de-escalation (Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, Trump-Putin peace signals, Iran war reluctance) has reduced oil volatility and equity risk premium, boosting dividend payers and steady-state earners (JPM, GS, AXP). However, the Fed remains hawkish (Schmid, Daly both reiterating inflation priority), and Treasury markets are showing leverage stress with yields extended sharply since Feb-Mar lows. The real test is Friday's NFP report—any beat could trigger selling into the strength, while a miss could trigger a risk-off move. Move is already underway; Dow has rallied into resistance near 51,300.
catalysts_ahead: US Jobs Report (NFP) – Friday 5 June 08:30 ET – could flip bias if beat or miss significantly
Fed speakers (Schmid, Daly continue hawkish narrative) – intraday risk
Treasury auction dynamics (10y yield vulnerability at 4.68%+) – overnight/Friday
Earnings season fade – select Dow components (UNH, HD, V) still reporting, could derail rally if misses mount
SpaceX IPO (SPCX, debuts next week) – ongoing rotation signal for capital flows
screening.risk_warning: If Friday's NFP prints hot (>200k, unemployment <4.0%), Fed holds hawkish stance, and Treasury yields spike above 4.75%, the Dow could reverse sharply despite today's rally—financials are rate-sensitive. Alternatively, private credit stress (Blackstone's redemption cap) could cascade into broad credit market dislocation, pulling equities lower. Additionally, any escalation in US-Iran tensions or fresh Hezbollah rejection of ceasefire could re-ignite oil and reverse the geopolitical tailwind.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is already +930pts mid-session (PHASE_2); wait for pullback to 51,100–51,150 support or retest of intraday highs near 51,300 before adding longs. Current levels are extended into resistance; avoid chasing into close ahead of Friday's NFP.

confirmation.entry_guidance: Rally already underway (+930pts intraday per news); wait for pullback to 51,000-51,200 support zone before adding long exposure.

factors.name: Intraday Price Action
factors.summary: Dow surging +930 points (+1.84%) with 25 of 30 components higher, on pace for record close per Investinglive 17:01. Broad-based buying, not narrow leadership.

factors.name: Sector Rotation into Financials
factors.summary: Financials leading: Goldman Sachs +4.93%, JPMorgan +3.64%, Amex +4.41%. Rotation out of underperforming semiconductors (Broadcom disappointment) into cyclical Dow names.

factors.name: Geopolitical De-escalation
factors.summary: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire announced (BBC 14:46), reducing risk premium. Putin signals readiness for Ukraine peace deal (Investinglive 17:21). However, Hezbollah rejected truce (Bloomberg 16:11), creating uncertainty.

factors.name: Oil Price Decline
factors.summary: Oil snaps 3-day win streak, down ~3% on ceasefire news and Trump reluctance to restart Iran war per WSJ (US Top News 14:00). Lowers input costs for Dow industrials, benefits consumer discretionary.

factors.name: Fed Commentary - Hawkish Tilt
factors.summary: Fed's Schmid: 'Biggest risk is now inflation' (Investinglive 17:41). Daly: 'Inflation remains top priority' (FXStreet 17:05). Hawkish tone suggests higher-for-longer rates, headwind for rate-sensitive Dow components.

factors.name: Treasury Market Stress
factors.summary: MarketWatch 16:46: 'War-weary Treasury market faces fresh test with Friday's jobs report' - investors demanding higher compensation to lend to US government. UK 10yr yields rose 97bps vs US 76bps, signaling global debt concern.

factors.name: Tech Sector Weakness
factors.summary: Broadcom set to shed $300bn on disappointing AI results; Micron flirts with record market-cap wipeout. However, Dow has lower tech weight than SPY, and rally is driven by non-tech names, making this less relevant.

factors.name: Employment Data Incoming
factors.summary: Treasury Sec Bessent: 'I wish job market had come out today' (Investinglive 17:20). NFP Friday is major catalyst. Long-term unemployment surging per US Top News 17:30, but recent data shows labor market firming per Daly comments.

factors.name: Dollar Weakness
factors.summary: USD weakening on ceasefire news, benefiting Dow multinationals with ~50% international revenue exposure. EUR/USD up 0.25%, GBP/USD +0.16% per FXStreet reports.

factors.name: Consumer & Economic Concerns
factors.summary: High gas prices affecting consumer behavior (MarketWatch 16:52 on dating app radius narrowing). Long-term unemployment surging with hidden economic costs (US Top News 17:30). Potential drag on Dow consumer-facing names.

factors.name: IPO Market Strength
factors.summary: Quantinuum valued $17bn, up 13%; Innio +15%; Liftoff +21%; Sunshine Silver +11%. Strong IPO market signals risk appetite and capital market health, supportive of broader equity momentum.

factors.name: Private Markets Liquidity Concerns
factors.summary: Blackstone capped withdrawals from $79bn BCRED fund as redemptions rose to 10% (US Top News 15:07, Bloomberg 17:14). Private asset liquidity fears reemerging, potential contagion risk to broader markets.

confirmation.narrative: Dow is experiencing broad-based rally driven by sector rotation from tech into financials/industrials, aided by geopolitical de-escalation (Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, Ukraine peace signals) and falling oil prices. The move is already well underway with 25 of 30 components green and +930pts intraday. However, hawkish Fed commentary, Treasury market stress ahead of Friday NFP, and Hezbollah's rejection of the ceasefire create downside risks. The rally appears to be Phase 2 of a risk-on rotation out of stretched mega-cap tech.
catalysts_ahead: US Non-Farm Payrolls Friday (June 5) - major catalyst per Treasury Sec and Fed comments
Geopolitical stability of Israel-Lebanon ceasefire given Hezbollah rejection
Fed speakers into NFP (potential further hawkish guidance)
Treasury auctions and bond market stability given stress signals
Potential China data releases (relevant to CAT, BA, industrials exposure)
confirmation.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Friday NFP shows unexpectedly hot labor market, reinforcing Fed hawkishness and spiking yields; (2) Geopolitical re-escalation if Israel-Lebanon ceasefire fully collapses; (3) Break below 51,000 on heavy volume signals failed breakout; (4) Private market liquidity concerns (Blackstone) spread to broader credit markets.

reason: DISAGREEMENT: Haiku=BUY vs Sonnet=BULLISH — silent log
